5-tech-spec.summary: Tech spec covers 5 phases (database, auth core, routes+UI, permissions, WebSocket auth). 17 new files, 15 modified. Key decisions: bcryptjs with SHA-256 pre-hash, hashed session tokens, cookie-based Socket.IO auth via io.use() middleware, requireAuth wrapper pattern, ProjectMember table for permissions.

8-test-plan.summary: 54 test cases across 5 phases. Unit: 32, Integration: 6, Component/UI: 8, WebSocket: 8. 41 P0 tests, 13 P1 tests. All 14 P0 and 5 P1 requirements covered (100%). Key security coverage: anti-enumeration, cookie flags (HttpOnly/SameSite/no-Secure), SHA-256 pre-hash boundary, crypto.randomUUID absence, WebSocket handshake auth, account lockout, session token hashing.

9-implementation.summary: All 5 phases implemented. 17 new files created, 15+ files modified. 85 new test cases written (phases 1-3). 426/426 tests pass. Known blocker: Prisma Accelerate proxy blocks DDL — auth DB tables must be applied manually via direct Postgres connection.

history.notes: Feature pipeline initialized — account authentication with password auth, group/permission extensibility

PRD and decisions.md created. Covers registration, login/session, route protection, project-level permissions, user groups. Research conducted on TanStack Start auth patterns and password hashing best practices.
Adversarial PRD review started -- Devil's Advocate + User Advocate lenses.

Verdict: REVISIONS. 3 blocking (email enumeration contradiction, missing WebSocket auth requirement, missing password max length + session recovery flow + permission revocation UX), 8 major, 4 minor findings. Total: 15 issues across both lenses.

PRD v2: Addressed all 5 blocking + 8 major + 4 minor issues from Nemesis review. Key changes: resolved email enumeration contradiction (anti-enum everywhere), added AUTH-GUARD-04 for WebSocket auth, added 128-char password max, defined session expiry recovery flow (5.6), defined permission revocation UX (AUTH-PERM-05), deferred groups to separate iteration, added Role Capabilities table, promoted rate limiting to in-scope, added empty states, accessibility requirements, and forgotten-password persona.

Re-review verdict: APPROVED. All 5 blocking and 8 major issues from initial review resolved. 4 minor informational findings remain (anti-enum UX friction, rate limiting lockout boundary, shared-project visual indicator, registration redirect confusion). None block tech spec.

4 gray areas resolved: session token storage (hashed — cookie raw, DB SHA-256), lockout model (fields on User), CollaborationSession migration (delete all rows, non-nullable FK from day one), TanStack Start + Socket.IO integration (deferred to Hephaestus with PoC requirement). context.md written.

Tech spec created. 5 phases, 17 new files, 15 modified files. Key decisions: bcryptjs with SHA-256 pre-hash (not Argon2id — native addon risk on Bun), hashed session tokens (SHA-256 in DB, raw in cookie), requireAuth wrapper pattern (not TanStack middleware), ProjectMember table (not ProjectPermission), Socket.IO auth via io.use() handshake middleware reading session cookie from headers. Vinxi/Nitro plugin API research confirmed: not needed — Socket.IO attaches to bare httpServer via existing Vite plugin.

Verdict: APPROVED. All 14 P0 and 5 P1 requirements covered. All 8 user flows aligned. All 4 locked decisions from context.md respected. 5 minor/informational findings (empty state copy detail, role capabilities mapping, forgotten password help text, session cleanup trigger, HTTPS warning detail) — none require revision.

Verdict: SOUND. No critical or high-severity issues. 1 medium (expired session cleanup lacks scheduled caller), 2 low (registration timing side-channel, file inventory count mismatch). Architecture is well-structured, all locked decisions respected, security model appropriate for LAN threat profile. Both reviews pass — stage 8 (test plan) set to ready.
